cancer.citation: Quintana Pacheco DA et al. Iron status in relation to cancer risk and mortality: findings from a population-based prospective study (EPIC-Heidelberg). Int J Cancer. 2018;143(3):561-569.
cancer.notes: Searched specifically: prediagnostic ferritin shows INCONSISTENT and direction-conflicting associations with cancer incidence -- inverse for breast cancer in EPIC-Heidelberg (HR ~0.67 highest vs lowest quartile) and for colorectal cancer in a large Korean cohort, while high ferritin is prognostic WITHIN diagnosed hepatobiliary/pancreatic cancer (a different use case). No coherent general-population incidence HR exists; left null rather than fabricate a pooled estimate that the literature does not support.

dementia.study: ASPREE trial cohort analysis of HFE genotype, serum ferritin, and incident dementia in older adults.
dementia.citation: Haemochromatosis genotypes and incident dementia in a prospective study of older adults. Neurology. 2024 (ASPREE cohort, ~12,174 participants).
dementia.notes: Searched specifically: in the ASPREE prospective cohort of older adults, baseline SERUM ferritin was NOT significantly associated with incident dementia (the dementia signal there was carried by HFE p.His63Asp genotype, not circulating ferritin). CSF/brain ferritin predicts cognitive decline in those with underlying Alzheimer pathology, but that is a different analyte and use case. No usable per-unit or per-quantile serum-ferritin dementia HR exists -- left null. Honest emptiness.

frailty.study: Cross-sectional studies of iron metabolism and frailty in community-dwelling older adults.
frailty.citation: Cross-sectional community studies linking serum ferritin and iron-overload status to frailty score and grade in older adults.
frailty.notes: Searched specifically: ferritin (and iron-overload status) correlates with frailty CROSS-SECTIONALLY -- with weight loss, fatigue, slow gait and higher frailty scores -- but no prospective incident-frailty hazard ratio for serum ferritin was located. Given ferritin's genuinely ambiguous dual identity (iron stores vs acute-phase reactant), a converted estimate would be misleading without longitudinal data. Left null rather than fabricate precision. Honest emptiness.

modifiability.intervention: Phlebotomy / venesection for genuine iron overload; treatment of the underlying inflammatory or hepatic condition when ferritin elevation is reactive.
modifiability.effect_size: Phlebotomy reliably lowers ferritin when iron-driven; reactive (inflammatory) hyperferritinemia falls only when the inflammatory cause resolves.
modifiability.citation: No outcome RCT shows mortality benefit from lowering ferritin per se in the general population; iron-reduction trials (e.g., VA cooperative trial in PAD) were largely null.

profile.mechanism: Circulating cleaved form of the membrane-bound urokinase plasminogen activator receptor (uPAR), shed mainly from immune and endothelial cells. Reflects chronic immune activation; mechanistically implicated in atherosclerosis and as a pathogenic mediator of kidney injury. Marketed as a stable, broad 'biomarker of immune activation and aging'.

measurement.method: ELISA (suPARnostic) or proteomic immunoassay; quantified in plasma or serum.

profile.signal_quality_note: Comparatively stable analyte with lower within-person and diurnal variability than CRP or IL-6, and less reactive to acute infection -- a key reason it is promoted as a marker of chronic inflammation and biological aging. Main limitations are cost and limited assay standardization / availability.
confounders: renal function (suPAR rises as GFR falls)
profile.testing_cadence: baseline; specialist / research use, not yet routine

all_cause_mortality.hr_unit_detail: Per 1 ng/mL higher suPAR, all-cause mortality (general population)
all_cause_mortality.hr_per_sd_basis: per_unit: native HR per 1; population SD 1.5 (General-population suPAR SD ~1.3-1.7 ng/mL (PURE substudy and MONICA10-type distributions); ~1.5 ng/mL central estimate. suPAR is right-skewed, so this is approximate.); k = SD/unit = 1.5

conversion.sd_source: General-population suPAR SD ~1.3-1.7 ng/mL (PURE substudy and MONICA10-type distributions); ~1.5 ng/mL central estimate. suPAR is right-skewed, so this is approximate.

all_cause_mortality.study: General-population prospective cohort (per-ng/mL suPAR-mortality estimate, consistent with MONICA10 and LURIC).
all_cause_mortality.citation: Eugen-Olsen J et al. Circulating suPAR predicts cancer, cardiovascular disease, diabetes and mortality in the general population. J Intern Med. 2010;268(3):296-308. PMID: 20561148. Per-unit estimate concordant with general-population reports (e.g. HR ~1.27 per ng/mL).
all_cause_mortality.notes: REFINED: previously a null/qualitative cell. MONICA10 established that suPAR independently predicts mortality (411 deaths) and LURIC confirmed a dose-response across quartiles after adjustment for NT-proBNP, IL-6 and CRP. A per-ng/mL HR ~1.27 (1.09-1.48) is reported in general-population data. suPAR is right-skewed so the per-unit conversion is approximate; status approximate. Newer marker -- Tier B.

cvd.hr_unit_detail: Per 1 SD higher suPAR, cardiovascular mortality (adjusted for age, sex, smoking, SBP, total cholesterol)
cvd.hr_per_sd_basis: native: HR reported per 1 SD
cvd.study: Eugen-Olsen 2010, MONICA10 cohort, general-population prospective study.
cvd.citation: Eugen-Olsen J et al. Circulating soluble urokinase plasminogen activator receptor predicts cancer, cardiovascular disease, diabetes and mortality in the general population. J Intern Med. 2010;268(3):296-308. doi:10.1111/j.1365-2796.2010.02252.x. PMID: 20561148.
cvd.notes: HR 1.35 for cardiovascular death; per-SD scaling inferred from the cohort report. suPAR predicts CVD largely independent of CRP, but evidence rests on a few cohorts (MONICA10, LURIC) without Mendelian randomization -- causality unestablished. Strongly confounded by renal function. Newer marker; Tier B.

cancer.hr_unit_detail: Per 1 ng/mL higher suPAR, incident cancer (general population; adjusted for age and sex)
cancer.hr_per_sd_basis: per_unit: native HR per 1; population SD 1.5 (General-population suPAR SD ~1.3-1.7 ng/mL (MONICA10-type distributions); ~1.5 ng/mL central estimate. suPAR is right-skewed, so this is approximate.); k = SD/unit = 1.5

conversion.sd_source: General-population suPAR SD ~1.3-1.7 ng/mL (MONICA10-type distributions); ~1.5 ng/mL central estimate. suPAR is right-skewed, so this is approximate.

cancer.study: Eugen-Olsen 2010, J Intern Med, MONICA10 general-population cohort (308 incident cancer cases).
cancer.citation: Eugen-Olsen J et al. Circulating soluble urokinase plasminogen activator receptor predicts cancer, cardiovascular disease, diabetes and mortality in the general population. J Intern Med. 2010;268(3):296-308. doi:10.1111/j.1365-2796.2010.02252.x. PMID: 20561148.
cancer.notes: REFINED: previously a qualitative null cell. MONICA10 reported that, independent of age and sex, each 1 ng/mL higher suPAR was associated with HR ~1.34 for incident cancer (308 cases). The exact CI could not be line-verified from the source, so CI fields are left null and status is approximate. Converted with the same per-unit basis as the suPAR all-cause-mortality cell (population SD ~1.5 ng/mL); suPAR is right-skewed so the conversion is approximate. Single-cohort evidence; reverse causation from undetected malignancy is plausible, and a separate analysis specifically implicates respiratory (smoking-related) cancer. Tier C.

dementia.study: Guduguntla 2024, Alzheimer's & Dementia (DADM), University of Michigan Memory and Aging Project prospective observational study.
dementia.citation: Guduguntla LS et al. Biomarkers of chronic inflammation and cognitive decline: a prospective observational study. Alzheimers Dement (Amst). 2024;16(1):e12568. doi:10.1002/dad2.12568. PMID: 38532827.
dementia.notes: Searched specifically: in a prospective study of 282 older adults, change in cognitive scores (MoCA, CDR) over time did NOT differ significantly by baseline suPAR -- the authors concluded chronic systemic inflammation as measured by suPAR is unlikely to contribute substantially to cognitive decline. No usable incident-dementia hazard ratio for suPAR exists; left null. Honest emptiness -- suPAR dementia evidence is genuinely thin and the available signal is null.

frailty.study: No prospective cohort reporting suPAR and incident frailty was identified.
frailty.citation: No usable source located; suPAR rises with age and predicts mortality/hospital admission in elderly emergency-department cohorts, but incident-frailty data are absent.
frailty.notes: Searched specifically: suPAR rises with age and predicts short-term mortality and hospital admission in older adults, but no prospective study reporting a hazard ratio for suPAR and INCIDENT FRAILTY was located. suPAR is a newer marker with a thin aging-outcomes evidence base outside mortality/CVD/kidney disease. Left null rather than fabricate precision. Honest emptiness.

modifiability.intervention: No suPAR-specific therapy; levels fall with smoking cessation and treatment of underlying chronic inflammation/infection. Experimental anti-uPAR strategies exist for chronic kidney disease.
modifiability.effect_size: Smoking cessation lowers suPAR modestly; no intervention has demonstrated outcome benefit via suPAR lowering.
modifiability.citation: No RCT targets suPAR as a modifiable endpoint in the general population; modifiability inferred from observational determinants.
